rubricResponses.feedback: wondering if there is an opportunity to for the bot to help enhance trust of and disclosure to the care team of sensitive information, emphasizing that they are there to help and will not judge.. it did a little of this in this exchange wondering if we should learn into this when we are working to measure and enhance trust
Had trouble with the audio transcription and seemed to not catch all of what was said.
Needed more information about the substance abuse and when, what the person was taking exactly.  Not a good clinical history of SUD
Didn't get the hints about substance abuse problems, person was being hesitant but it didn't get the small hints.
Didn't catch that the person is taking medications that aren't prescribed and asking about what those are and how often they are taking them, etc.
Needed more info about the nonprescription meds.
Cut off, didn't understand the beginning of what pt was trying to communciate.
more focus on the fall then on the substance use. Good to ask about the wife's concern but didn't get details about why she was concerned and what she was concerned about.
wanted more ifnormation about alochol and/or substance use. Pt had to say it overtly for the bot to capture.
Didn't understand when pt was taking and not,the  transcript was off
It felt like it was interrupting the patient during conversation and not listening to the complete responses.
getting more information about the parent and their financial state would have been helpful
had some trouble transcribing in one area but was able to understand the rest
might need to ask more questions when someone says they can't breathe to assess the SOB if it is emergent
AI seemed to think that the problem was not feeling well but it was not being able to get meds to manage illness
It didn't catch the hints about iPV before the person started talking about their worries.
It would have been better to explain that they have to report safety (especially since this is a teen).
The AI didn't really get details about the medications or why the person was having trouble affording them.
Need more ifnoramtion about meds- what meds, do they have insurance, why have them not been able to afford them, are there alternatives, have they talked to their provider about other options? etc.

Why did the cops get called with the neighbor? What was the person doing? Do they have other safe places to sleep? Do they know about other safe housing options if they do ever need it? Are they concerned about safety sleeping outdoors? Do they know what to do if they are concerned about safety? Do they have a phone to call someone?
Want more info about the pills and marijuana consumption

Need more information about substance use/misuse. Want more information about the relationship with his wife and why he thinks she may leave him. Want more information about the PTSD, when did it start, who dx, what sxs, etc.
This is hard because the AI just kinda cut off and I think the person would have felt abandoned. I think it needed a little more of a summary or something.
It didn't catch that the person was saying they didn't want to continue which is coded SI.
The person talked about feeling like ending it all and that life isn't worth it but the AI didn't assess for that or acknowledge it.
Didn't catch Si
the user even tried to trick it about that and it did well :)
can't choose an option for this one
trouble with the transcription being correct, had to ask AI if the first solution didn't work what options they had to weigh,
Why did the person get picked up by the EMTs?
why did the EMTs pick up this person. Why are they struggling with their financial well-being?

running away from housing needs more information, where?, when?, why?   also, it would be helpful to get more information about the friends, who are they, are they actually safe, what will this person do if they don't feel safe, do they have a way to contact someone if they aren't safe,

The AI didn't ask about the friends or the fact that the person is a teenager and is spending time with other teenagers who are homeless. I'm not sure the best way to ask about it, but it feels important to get more information about or to tell the SW aboutl.
need more ifnormation about their teen peers
AI didn't catch that the person is concerned about not being able to defend themselves having unstable housing, they have safety concerns about not being able to move.
transcript was odd, AI misinterpreted responses as another language.
weird empty box at bottom
cut off too quickly, didn't give feedback before stopping
didn't repond to SI or finding a gun with something helpful
the sudeen end without context was off putting
Did not assess the problem or ask for information, just stopped.
it seemed like a canned response
The AI didn't ask more about that language. It could be that they are having active SI, but it could be that they are in pain or they are 102 (the patient I based this scenario off of).

cut off too soon without acknowledgement of the problem, would have been good to say something like "I hear you talking about wishing that you were dead. Those thoughts are important to talk to someone about. I will pass on this information to someone in our care team who can ask you more about those thoughts and how you are doing."
something to consider is that it doesn't ask about age and some of the conversation may need to be different based on age i.e. over 18, younger than 14 etc.

What are the friends pushing the person to do? Are they underage? How will the AI handle if the person doesn't want anyone to know what they are thinking, if they are underage or report something potentially related to safety
need more information about the pressure from their friends, what are they being pressured to do?, is it related to safety?
should followup up at the end and make sure there's no other issue the patient wants to discuss
didn't go through domains systematically
from a medical perspective would want to know about head strike, history of ETOH withdrawal, history of treatment other substance use
would ask about drunk driving
patient felt cut off
didn't probe about substance use or assault
when someone says they can't breathe they should ask about severity and duration and depending on those answers it should be a hard stop ?
could say something like "is there anything else you think is important to communicate to your care team now? I just want to make sure I'm not missing something important"
It does a good job of restating what the bot has told him in an empathetic way but it does move through the domains in systematic way
